title: [InstCombine] Add exact flags for ext idiom `shr (shl X, Y), Y`
summary:
The generalization strategy evolved from a specific `icmp` pattern to a broader `shl`/`lshr`/`ashr` identity. Initially, the optimization recognized `icmp eq (and (lshr (shl -1, Y), Y), X), X` as always true. Developers expanded this by identifying the core property: `lshr (shl X, Y), Y` (and similarly for `ashr`) always yields exact bits when shifting by the same amount, regardless of the shifted value or shift type. This allows setting the `exact` flag on the second shift, enabling further optimizations.

The patch implements this by adding a simple pattern match in `setShiftFlags`: if a shift’s operand is a shift by the same amount in the opposite direction, mark it as `exact`. This minimal change leverages existing exact-flag propagation, turning a special-case `icmp` fold into a general-purpose shift-pair improvement that benefits many downstream transforms.
